div1.entryFree:
Blade, 1) the green shoot of corn before it grows to seed; used as an emblem of youth: done in the b. of youth, All's V, 3, 6 (M. Edd. blaze, on account of the following simile).
2) the cutting part of a weapon: you break jests as braggarts do their --s, Ado V, 1, 190. H6A II, 4, 13. H6C I, 3, 50. Rom. I, 4, 84. Mcb. II, 1, 46. V, 8, 11. Used for the whole sword: he shakes aloft his Roman b. Lucr. 505. Mids. V, 147. Mids. V, 147 R3 I, 4, 211. Rom. I, 1, 85.
3) a fencer: a very good b.! a very tall man! Rom. II, 4, 31 (expressions ridiculed by Mercutio)
